subject: [PATCH v3 2/2] mm/vmscan: reduce lru_lock contention via vmstat-derived scan-balance cost

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary:
Reviewer requested that the patch description be rewritten to focus on explaining the existing code, rather than providing an in-depth explanation of its functionality.

Reviewer Johannes Weiner suggested simplifying the delta extraction in cost_accum[] and performing weight calculations directly on the extracted delta to ensure correct overflow behavior.

Reviewer Johannes Weiner questioned the need to check individual vmstat counters between reclaim cycles, citing that there are no scans->rotations and refault events cannot exceed ULONG_MAX

Reviewer Johannes Weiner suggested a rewritten version of the code, proposing to use a struct lru_cost to accumulate rotation and IO costs for anon and file LRUs, and then halve the accumulated counts until they are within a certain bound.
sentiment_signals: suggestion to stick closer to original comments

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch reduces contention on the lru_lock by replacing producer-side accumulators with a read-side accumulator fed from per-LRU vmstat counters. The old system used two scalars in struct lruvec to accumulate costs, which was causing heavy contention on memory-heavy workloads. The new approach uses existing vmstat counters and adds explicit node_stat counters for the remaining inputs. This change allows the cost signals to be recomputed without holding the lru_lock, reducing contention and improving performance.
